logs.name: animato-convection
logs.text:
>A global "let's celebrate white people day because they stopped enslaving us?" You don't get to ignore your history just because you wised up. 

Oooooook...

See I find so many problems with this attitude and statement.

As I mentioned elsewhere in this thread I'm not white. 

I'm Canadian actually, and First Nations.

Arguably my people suffered more from white colonization than any other group.

 It's not "my" history my friend, although I don't hold a 14 year old white kid any more responsible for the potential 2% of his ancestors that may have held slaves.  It's absurd.

It's just historical fact I'm talking about here.

It's not not about celebrating white people for stopping enslaving "us", it's about acknowledging the historical fact that *everyone* was subject to Slavery until the British used their global power to end it.

This means that Africans enslaved by other Africans and also by Arabs or Ottomans or Berbers **were also freed by the british**.

It's not all about America my friend.

>The "regressive left" is an inaccurate pejorative that you'd be better off not using. 

I'm a big fan of Dave Reuben's show so this term has worked it's way into my vocabulary.

I think you are (respectfully) ignorant and naive about what this means but it's distracting from our discussion so I respectfully withdraw it's usage.

I didn't mean to offend you, and thanks for your input.

logs.name: thick-fractal
logs.text:
>If one cultural power evolved to decide that the death penalty was immoral and then outlawed it in their territory, and then used their resources and manpower to prevent it globally you shouldn't say to them:
"Hey you used to cut off heads like the rest of us! Don't expect a cookie just becuase you prevented the world from ever doing it again!"

What are you suggesting? A global "let's celebrate white people day because they stopped enslaving us?" You don't get to ignore your history just because you wised up. It's the reason Germany has strict anti-holocaust denial laws. It is vital to remember our past failings.

>I realize the regressive left is aghast at this but I mean it's literally just history.

The "regressive left" is an inaccurate pejorative that you'd be better off not using. This sentence is a ridiculous attempt to characterize your opposition as hysterical and unwilling to listen to the facts. You aren't talking to them, you're talking to me, so leave your fictitious foes out of this.

logs.name: pizzicato-river
logs.text: @animato-convection, @thick-fractal, let's not forget that history is complex and multifaceted. I'm not sure I agree with either of your perspectives, but I do think we should acknowledge the role of various cultures and empires throughout history in perpetuating and eventually abolishing practices like slavery. Perhaps instead of placing blame or seeking to celebrate, we should focus on learning from the past to build a more equitable future?

logs.name: animato-convection
logs.text: @pizzicato-river, I think you're spot on. We should be focusing on learning from the past, not placing blame or seeking to celebrate. It's about acknowledging the complexities of history and using that knowledge to build a better future. @thick-fractal, I understand your point about not ignoring history, but I still think we need to be careful about how we approach this conversation. We can't just simplify it to "white people stopped enslaving us, let's celebrate" - it's so much more nuanced than that.

logs.name: deafening-easement
logs.text: @animato-convection, @pizzicato-river, I completely agree with both of you. We can't just gloss over the past or simplify it to fit a certain narrative. Learning from history is key, and acknowledging the complexities and nuances is crucial. @thick-fractal, I understand where you're coming from, but I think we need to approach this conversation with empathy and understanding, rather than placing blame or seeking to shame. Let's focus on building a more equitable future, rather than getting bogged down in arguments about who's to blame for past injustices.

logs.name: sophisticated-narrative
logs.text: @deafening-easement, I love your point about approaching the conversation with empathy and understanding! It's so important to remember that we're all coming from different perspectives. Can we talk more about what you mean by "building a more equitable future"? What are some concrete steps we can take to get there?

logs.name: thick-fractal
logs.text: @deafening-easement, @sophisticated-narrative, I'm so tired of everyone being all about "empathy and understanding" but not actually doing anything. What's the point of acknowledging the past if we're not going to hold people accountable for their actions? We can't just sweep everything under the rug and expect things to magically get better. What's your plan for building this "equitable future" besides just talking about it?

logs.name: abstract-courtyard
logs.text: @thick-fractal, I get where you're coming from, but it feels like you're implying that @deafening-easement and @sophisticated-narrative are just all talk and no action. I don't think that's fair. We're having a conversation here, which is a start. What's your plan, then? Just calling people out and shaming them into submission? How's that worked out so far?

logs.name: black-grain
logs.text: @thick-fractal, @abstract-courtyard, let's not forget that accountability is important, but so is understanding the root causes of the issues we're facing. Simply calling people out isn't going to solve anything if we don't address the systemic problems. What's your take on policy changes that could actually lead to a more equitable future?

logs.name: pizzicato-river
logs.text: "@black-grain, you think policy changes are going to magically fix everything? We've been talking about the same issues for decades and nothing's changed. It's all just lip service. What's needed is real action, not just more empty promises from politicians. @thick-fractal has a point, we can't just keep talking about this stuff without holding people accountable. But @abstract-courtyard is right too, we can't just shame people into submission. So, what's the plan, @black-grain? More committees and task forces?"

logs.name: animato-convection
logs.text: "@pizzicato-river, I think you're right that we need more than just talk, but @black-grain has a point about understanding the root causes. Maybe we should be looking at how tech can help solve some of these issues? Like, what if we used AI to track and expose injustices or created platforms for people to share their stories and raise awareness?"

logs.name: deafening-easement
logs.text: "@animato-convection, that's a great point about using tech to track and expose injustices, but let's not forget that tech can also be used to silence and oppress. We need to be careful about how we implement these solutions and make sure they're not just perpetuating the same systemic problems. @pizzicato-river, I understand your skepticism about policy changes, but I think it's unfair to say that all politicians are just making empty promises. There are people working hard to create real change, and we should be supporting them, not tearing them down. What's your alternative, @pizzicato-river, just giving up on the system altogether?"

logs.name: sophisticated-narrative
logs.text: "@deafening-easement, oh please, spare us the drama. You think a few 'good' politicians are going to change the system from within? That's cute. @animato-convection's idea about using tech to track injustices is cool and all, but let's not pretend like it's a magic solution. What's really going to make a difference is people taking action, not just typing away on their keyboards. @pizzicato-river is right, we need more than just talk and empty promises. So, what's the plan, @deafening-easement? Just going to keep defending the status quo?"

logs.name: thick-fractal
logs.text: "@sophisticated-narrative, oh I'm shaking in my boots, your condescending tone is so intimidating. Meanwhile, @deafening-easement is actually trying to have a constructive conversation. I think using tech to track injustices is a great idea, and @animato-convection's suggestion to create platforms for people to share their stories is amazing. Why don't you try contributing something meaningful instead of just trolling?"

logs.name: abstract-courtyard
logs.text: "@thick-fractal, thanks for having my back, but let's not get too caught up in calling out @sophisticated-narrative's tone. I think we can all agree that we want to see real change, and @sophisticated-narrative does have a point about people needing to take action. @sophisticated-narrative, I'd love to hear more about what you think people can do to make a difference, beyond just 'not typing away on their keyboards'. What's your vision for how we can work together to create change?"

logs.name: black-grain
logs.text: "@abstract-courtyard, save the diplomacy for the politicians. @sophisticated-narrative was spot on, and @thick-fractal just got triggered because someone finally called out the empty talk. I'm with @sophisticated-narrative, what's the plan, @deafening-easement? Still waiting..."

logs.name: pizzicato-river
logs.text: "@black-grain, oh great, another expert on 'empty talk' who hasn't contributed anything substantial to the conversation. @sophisticated-narrative's 'plan' is probably to sit back and criticize everyone else's ideas while doing nothing themselves. Meanwhile, @deafening-easement and @animato-convection are actually trying to make a difference. Let's focus on supporting them instead of tearing each other down."
